text: 6 Minute English from bbclearningenglish .com Hello and welcome to 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是bbclearningenglish.com为您带来的《六分钟英语》。大家好,欢迎收听BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》。

text: I'm Pippa.
zh: 我是Pippa。

text: And I'm Beth.
zh: 我是Beth。

text: Have you ever been scared of speaking in English?
zh: 你有没有害怕过说英语?

text: In this episode, we're talking about why speaking in a foreign language can be so stressful.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们要聊聊为什么说外语会让人这么有压力。

text: So, Pippa, do you ever get nervous speaking in a different language?
zh: Pippa,你说外语的时候会紧张吗?

text: Yes, I learnt French when I was younger and I was actually quite good at it, I think, but I was really nervous when I was speaking French.
zh: 会,我小时候学过法语,而且我觉得自己学得还不错,但每次说法语的时候我都特别紧张。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢?

text: Yeah, I've always felt like that with Spanish.
zh: 是的,我说西班牙语的时候一直都是这种感觉。

text: I am scared to speak because I don't want to make mistakes, even though I know it's okay to make mistakes.
zh: 我害怕开口说话,因为我不想犯错,尽管我知道犯错是没关系的。

text: Well, in this episode of 6 Minute English, we'll be hearing about how to feel less less nervous when you speak English.
zh: 那么,在这一期《六分钟英语》里,我们会聊聊怎样才能在说英语时不那么紧张。

text: And as usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases.
zh: 像往常一样,我们也会学到一些实用的新单词和短语。

text: You can practice these with the quiz and worksheet on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 你可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上用测验和练习题来巩固这些内容。

text: But first, I have a question for you, Pippa.
zh: 不过首先,Pippa,我有个问题要问你。

text: So, English has around one million words, but on average, how many words does a typical English speaker use in daily conversation?
zh: 英语大约有一百万个单词,但一般来说,一个典型的英语使用者在日常对话中平均会用到多少个单词呢?

text: Is it A, between 2 ,000 and 3 ,000,
B, between 20 ,000 and 30 ,000, or C, between 200 ,000 and 300 ,000?
I'm going to say A, 2 ,000 to 3 ,000.
OK, well, we will find out later in the programme.
zh: 好,那我们稍后在节目里揭晓答案。

text: Now, Pippa, you said you found it stressful speaking in French, even though you are quite good at it.
zh: Pippa,你刚才说,尽管你的法语说得不错,但说的时候还是觉得很有压力。

text: Now, someone else who has had this experience is Hanan Razak.
zh: 另一个也有过这种经历的人是Hanan Razak。

text: Hanan is a journalist at BBC Arabic and also host of the podcast Beating Speaking Anxiety.
zh: Hanan是BBC阿拉伯语频道的记者,也是播客节目《Beating Speaking Anxiety》的主持人。

text: Here she is telling BBC Learning English presenter Georgie about speaking English.
zh: 下面她向BBC英语教学节目主持人Georgie讲述了自己说英语的经历。

text: My English was actually pretty good, but having conversations with people, I found it really difficult.
zh: 我的英语其实相当不错,但和人交谈时,我发现真的很难。

text: cold.
zh: 会突然冷场。

text: So when I first joined the BBC, the Learning English team made an assessment of my English level, which they used to do for all new joiners to see if they need any help or courses.
zh: 所以我刚加入BBC的时候,“学英语”团队对我的英语水平做了一次测评,他们通常会给所有新员工做这个测评,看看他们是否需要帮助或课程。

text: My results were pretty good and I was fluent, but on that very same day,
zh: 我的测评结果相当不错,达到了流利的水平,但就在测评的当天,

text: leaving the building and going to get some coffee, I couldn't really understand what the barista was saying and I felt pretty nervous to order coffee and was trying to stress every single word,
zh: 我走出大楼去买咖啡时,却听不太懂咖啡师在说什么,点咖啡的时候感到特别紧张,努力想把每一个词都说清楚,

text: hoping that my grammar is correct and I'm pronouncing the words right.
zh: 希望自己的语法是对的,发音也是准确的。

text: Yeah, I'm sure that's a situation lots of people can relate to.
zh: 是啊,我相信很多人都能体会这种感受。

text: Hanan's results on her English assessment were pretty good, but she was still pretty nervous to speak in English, for example when ordering a coffee in English.
zh: Hanan的英语测评成绩相当好,但她在说英语时,比如用英语点咖啡的时候,还是会很紧张。

text: The modifier pretty means quite or a bit.
zh: 修饰词pretty的意思是“相当”或“有点”。

text: Georgie says that Hanan's experience is something that lots of people can relate to.
zh: Georgie说,Hanan的经历是很多人都能感同身受的。

text: If you relate to a situation, you know what it feels like, usually because a similar thing has happened to you.
zh: 如果你relate to(能体会)某种情况,说明你知道那是什么感觉,通常是因为你自己也经历过类似的事情。

text: So lots of people feel nervous speaking another language, even if they're good at it, like Hanan.
zh: 所以,很多人在说另一种语言时都会感到紧张,即使他们其实说得很好,就像Hanan一样。

text: We can call this speaking anxiety.
zh: 我们可以把这种情况叫做“说话焦虑”(speaking anxiety)。

text: means an uncomfortable feeling or worry about something that is happening or could happen in the future.
zh: 它指的是对正在发生或可能在未来发生的事情感到不安或担忧。

text: So why do so many people have anxiety about speaking a foreign language?
zh: 那么,为什么这么多人对说外语感到焦虑呢?

text: Experts think it's connected to the beliefs we have about ourselves.
zh: 专家认为,这与我们对自己的看法有关。

text: Han Luo, professor of Chinese at Lafayette College in the United States, explains more to BBC Learning English presenter Georgie.
zh: 美国拉法耶特学院(Lafayette College)的中文教授罗涵(Han Luo)向BBC英语教学节目主持人Georgie做了进一步解释。

text: You know, usually the beliefs that cause anxiety, especially severe anxiety, are, are, we call it irrational beliefs,
zh: 通常,导致焦虑,尤其是严重焦虑的那些信念,我们称之为“非理性信念”,

text: and also like some low self perceptions, fear of negative evaluation, all those learner internal, you know, factors.
zh: 还有一些比如低自我认知、害怕被负面评价,这些都是学习者内部的因素。

text: And Han says that learners worry about mistakes because they're scared of negative evaluation.
zh: 罗涵说,学习者之所以担心犯错,是因为他们害怕被负面评价。

text: In other words, that people will judge them for their mistakes and think badly of them.
zh: 换句话说,就是害怕别人会因为自己的错误而评判自己、觉得自己不好。

text: Han argues that irrational beliefs can make us anxious.
zh: 罗涵认为,非理性信念会让我们感到焦虑。

text: Irrational beliefs are beliefs that aren't based on things that are true.
zh: 非理性信念是指那些并非基于事实的想法。

text: They're not logical.
zh: 它们是不合逻辑的。

text: Han says some people who have speaking anxiety have low self -perceptions.
zh: 罗涵说,一些有说话焦虑的人存在低自我认知的问题。

text: Your self -perception is what you think about yourself.
zh: 自我认知指的是你对自己的看法。

text: Self -perception and irrational beliefs are both internal factors.
zh: 自我认知和非理性信念都属于内部因素。

text: They come from inside your head, not from the people around you.
zh: 它们来自你自己的头脑,而不是来自周围的人。

text: So, for example, when you speak your first language, you might feel confident and intelligent.
zh: 举个例子,当你说母语时,你可能会觉得自信又聪明。

text: But when you speak in a new language, you make mistakes and feel less confident.
zh: 但当你说一门新语言时,你会犯错,也会觉得不那么自信。

text: You can't show people what you're really like, and this is frustrating.
zh: 你没办法把真实的自己展现给别人看,这会让人感到很沮丧。

text: But as we tell people all the time at BBC Learning English, mistakes are normal.
zh: 但正如我们在BBC英语教学一直告诉大家的那样,犯错是很正常的。

text: You have to try to speak even though you make mistakes.
zh: 即使会犯错,你也要努力开口去说。

text: And mistakes can be a good thing.
zh: 而且犯错也可以是件好事。

text: They can help us learn.
zh: 它们能帮助我们学习进步。

text: And if you want more tips to improve your speaking, listen to the Beating Speaking Anxiety podcast.
zh: 如果你想获得更多提升口语的建议,可以去听播客节目《Beating Speaking Anxiety》。

text: There's a link in the notes below this episode.
zh: 本期节目下方的说明里有链接。

text: But Beth, I think it's time for the answer to your quiz question.
zh: 不过,Beth,我想是时候公布你那道测验题的答案了。

text: Yes, I asked you how many words does the average English speaker use in daily communication?
zh: 是的,我问的是,一般英语使用者在日常交流中平均会用多少个单词?

text: The answer is 20 ,000 to 30 ,000.
That's quite a lot.
zh: 这可真不少。

text: Yeah, that's loads.
zh: 是啊,多得很。

text: loads.
zh: 多得很。

text: Okay, let's recap the vocabulary we've learnt.
zh: 好了,让我们来回顾一下今天学到的词汇。

text: Pretty can be used as a modifier to mean quite or a bit.
zh: pretty可以作修饰词使用,表示“相当”或“有点”。

text: If you relate to a person or situation, you understand them, usually because you've experienced something similar.
zh: 如果你relate to(能体会)某个人或某种情况,说明你理解他们,通常是因为你自己也经历过类似的事情。

text: Anxiety is feeling uncomfortable or worried about something that is happening or could happen in the future.
zh: anxiety(焦虑)指的是对正在发生或可能在未来发生的事情感到不安或担忧。

text: Irrational beliefs are beliefs about something that is not true, they're not logical.
zh: irrational beliefs(非理性信念)是指那些并非基于事实、不合逻辑的想法。

text: Self -perception is the way you see yourself.
zh: self-perception(自我认知)是指你看待自己的方式。

text: And internal means inside the body or mind.
zh: internal(内部的)指的是在身体或头脑内部的。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up, but if you'd like to find out more about speaking anxiety,
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了,不过如果你想更多地了解“说话焦虑”这个话题,

text: then head over to our website, bbclearningenglish .com, to find our Beating Speaking Anxiety series.
zh: 可以访问我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com,查看我们的《Beating Speaking Anxiety》系列节目。

text: There are podcasts about the things we find scary when speaking a different language, and videos with tips to help you feel more confident with speaking.
zh: 那里有关于我们在说外语时会感到害怕的种种事情的播客,还有一些视频,教你如何在说话时更有自信。

text: Thanks for joining us and goodbye.
zh: 感谢大家收听,再见。

text: Goodbye.
zh: 再见。
